rulings.Case Title: 781 BEACH STREET PROPERTY L.P., A CALIFORNIA VS. PETER REMEDIOS, AN INDIVIDUAL ALSO KNOWN AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 10, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANTs REMEDIOS STUDIO LONG BEACH, INC.,CHHADA SIEMBIEDA REMEDIOS, INC.,, PETER REMEDIOS, REMEDIOS STUDIO SAN FRANCISCO, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and second causes of action and granted as to the third and fourth causes of action. Regarding the first and second causes of action, Defendants shifted the burden by showing that P cannot establish a contractual relationship with the non-tenant Defendants. (See Defendants' Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 20-21.) However, Plaintiff has raised a triable issue regarding whether the non-tenant Defendants are alter egos of the tenant Defendant. (See Plaintiff's Additional Material Facts A-F, I-J and L-O, S-U). Regarding the third and fourth cause of action, Defendants shifted the burden by showing that Defendants made no misrepresentations to Plaintiff. (See Defendants' Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 29-33.) Plaintiff fails to present evidence sufficient to raise a triable issue of fact. (See Plaintiff's Response to Defendants' Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 29-33.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK O'FLYNN et al VS. DOES 1 TO 20
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 10, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANT CLIFFORD FRIED's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first cause of action and sustained with leave to amend as to the second cause of action. On a demurrer, the Court only looks at the face of the complaint and, from the face of the Second Amended Complaint, the first cause of action is not time-barred. The second cause of action fails to allege: (1) Plaintiffs' performance or excuse for nonperformance of the contract; (2) Defendants' breach; and (3) resulting damage to Plaintiffs. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can in do so in good faith, to allege facts sufficient to constitute a breach of contract claim. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the requested documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SPECTOR & BENNETT, A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION VS. DAVID P. JONES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Relief From Protective Order To Take Deposition Of Yano L. Rubinstein; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 10, 2012, Line 23, PLAINTIFF SPECTOR & BENNETT, A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONs MOTION For Relief From Protective Order To Take Deposition Of Yano L. Rubinstein. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Kane as follows: Denied. Given the presumption against depositions of opposing counsel, the representation by Jones' counsel that Rubenstein will not be called as a witness, and the need for a showing of extreme good cause as set forth in the three factor test, Spector & Bennett has not has made a sufficient showing to overturn the protective order issued in February 2012. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DELASHON MONIQUE GREEN, SURVIVING SPOUSE OF et al VS. D.H.S. GLOBAL INVESTMENTS LLC, DOING BUSINESS AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Written Disco Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 10, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT TERRANCE ALAN'S Motion To Compel Written Discovery Responses. Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GLENN MENSING VS. LYNDON LIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Unverified Answer To Verified Complaint And Enter Default Or Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 10, 2012, Line 20, PLAINTIFF GLENN MENSING's Motion To Strike Unverified Answer To Verified Complaint And Enter Default Or Judgment. Denied. The motion is untimely. (Code Civ. Proc. § 435(b); Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1322.) However, the court strikes the answer, sua sponte, with 30 days leave to amend. (Code Civ. Proc. § 436(b).) An answer to a verified complaint must be verified. (Code Civ. Proc. § 446(a).) Further, Defendants Liu and De Los Reyes are not California licensed attorneys and thus cannot represent the corporate defendants, Stanford Asset Group, Inc. or Lakeshore Financial Services aka Lakeshore Financial Services, Inc. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MYERS VS. JENNY PEI LIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Frank Myers' Motion To Compel Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 10, 2012, Line 24, Plaintiff Frank Myers' Motion To Compel Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Kane as follows: Denied. The court previously ruled that defendant generally appeared on June 8, 2012. Since defendant was served on June 15, 2012 for a deposition on June 21, 2012, the notice was not timely because under CCP §2025.210(b), service of a deposition notice on a defendant cannot be made until 20 days after service of summons or appearance absent an order shortening time. (See O'Grady v. Superior Court (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 1423, 1454 [required to secure order to conduct discovery prior to 20 day period]; Bernson v. Browning-Ferris Industries (1994) 7 Cal.4th 926, 931 n.2 [to the same effect].) Sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
